name: Candyland Sports Complex
description: Candyland Sports Complex is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Longwood, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Central Winds Pickleball Courts
description: Central Winds Pickleball Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Winter Springs, FL. There are 14 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. You can reserve the courts. Players have access to restrooms and lights.

name: Eastmonte Park
description: Eastmonte Park is a place to play pickleball in Altamonte Springs, FL. There are 10 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free. Players have access to lights, restrooms, and water.

name: Groveview Park
description: Groveview Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sanford, FL. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Lake Hodge Park
description:
Beautiful local park
Full-size tennis court with Pickleball lines 
Group and private lessons available by appointed city approved Pickleball Coach

name: Lake Mary
description: Lake Mary is a place to play pickleball in Lake Mary, FL. There are 5 outdoor asphalt, hard, and concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Lee P Moore Park Tennis & Pickleball Courts
description: Come play pickleball at Lee P Moore Park Tennis & Pickleball Courts in Sanford, FL! There are 6 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free.

name: Oviedo Gymnasium and Aquatic Facility
description: Come play pickleball at Oviedo Gymnasium and Aquatic Facility in Oviedo, FL! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — Resident Member $3.00 per visit / Resident Non-Member	$5.00 per visit

name: Oviedo Pickleball Training Center
description: Air conditioned court, WiFi, parking, clean private restroom, 24/7 availability for members, refrigerator for cold drinks, water fountain, large screen TV with all sporting events and streaming options. Text 407-278-3014. Leagues and tournaments, lessons and intro clinics, youth camps and leagues
fees: Fee required — $50 to reserve air conditioned court

name: Remington Park
description: Come play pickleball at Remington Park in Oviedo, FL! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. The courts cannot be reserved.

name: Riverside Park
description: Courts are first come, first served except for coaches contracted with the city. For questions call 4079715575.

name: Round Lake Park
description:
Courts are usually reserved most evenings. A list of reserved courts and times for the current week can be found on a sheet posted at a small building near the court entrances.

There is no paddle system - it's first-come, first served (reservations being the exception).

Lessons are not permitted unless previously set up on a contract with recreation staff.

name: Sweetwater Oaks Club
description: These are private courts for residents of Sweetwater Oaks only.
fees: Members only — Residents of Sweetwater Oaks only.

name: Sylvan Lake Courts
description: Sylvan Lake Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sanford, FL. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private.

name: The Salvation Army Community Center
description: The Salvation Army Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Sanford, FL. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, wheelchair accessibility, and water.

name: Trotwood Park
description: Trotwood Park is a place to play pickleball in Winter Springs, FL. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Wedgewood Tennis Villas
description: Wedgewood Tennis Villas is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Winter Springs, FL. There are 8 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The facility includes amenities like lights. The courts are private.

name: Westmonte Park
description: The courts need to be reserved and a ranger will come around to check for the reservation name. Reserving online will provide you with a confirmation email. It is $5 per hour per court. There is a tennis court that can be divided into two pickleball courts (there is a moveable net on location). There are NO dogs allowed in the park!
open_play_schedule: Reservations can be made 2-7 days in advanced. Same reservations must be made with the office during business hours. After hours and on the weekend, it is cash only.
